title: Bosea humilis
taxonomy: Bosea humilis is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. It belongs to the genus Bosea, which is a small group of plants native to Africa. Bosea humilis is further classified as a member of the tribe Amarantheae. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf structure and seed production. The plant's classification is an important tool for botanists and taxonomists studying its evolution and relationships within the plant kingdom.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
